message.content:
You are editing AsciiDoc files for a cleanup pace. Make ALL of the following changes:

## 1. RBSA-SpecTop.adoc (`/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/lenses/RBSA-SpecTop.adoc`)

This is a large file. Read it in chunks as needed.

### Remove attribute references from mapping section:

Remove these lines from the mapping section (around lines 186, 200, 230-231, 253-254, 257-263):
```
:at_operational_machine:     <<at_operational_machine,Operational Machine>>
:at_stash_machine:           <<at_stash_machine,Stash Machine>>
:ops_rbv_check:              <<ops_rbv_check,RBV Check Operation>>
:ops_rbv_mirror:             <<ops_rbv_mirror,RBV Mirror Operation>>
:rbrr_registry_owner:        <<rbrr_registry_owner,RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER>>
:rbrr_registry_name:         <<rbrr_registry_name,RBRR_REGISTRY_NAME>>
:rbrr_ignite_machine_name:   <<rbrr_ignite_machine_name,RBRR_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME>>
:rbrr_deploy_machine_name:   <<rbrr_deploy_machine_name,RBRR_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME>>
:rbrr_crane_tar_gz:          <<rbrr_crane_tar_gz,RBRR_CRANE_TAR_GZ>>
:rbrr_manifest_platforms:    <<rbrr_manifest_platforms,RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS>>
:rbrr_chosen_podman_version: <<rbrr_chosen_podman_version,RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION>>
:rbrr_chosen_vmimage_origin: <<rbrr_chosen_vmimage_origin,RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN>>
:rbrr_chosen_identity:       <<rbrr_chosen_identity,RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY>>
```

### Replace the VM Management Operations section (around lines 877-893):

Remove the entire `=== VM Management Operations` section including the two subsections (ops_rbv_check and ops_rbv_mirror), and replace with:

```
=== Podman VM Supply Chain

include::RBSPV-PodmanVmSupplyChain.adoc[]
```

The section to remove spans from line 877 (`=== VM Management Operations`) through line 893 (`include::RBSVM-rbv_mirror.adoc[]`). Leave line 895 onward (the `[[mkc_interface_check]]` section) untouched.

### Remove definition anchors (around lines 1564-1573):

Remove these two term definitions:
```
[[at_stash_machine]]
{at_stash_machine}::
A temporary Podman virtual machine created with crane installed for performing registry queries and image operations.
Removed after completing its specific tasks.

[[at_operational_machine]]
{at_operational_machine}::
The persistent Podman virtual machine that hosts all
{at_bottle_service_s}
and their associated containers.
```

### Also search for and remove any [[rbrr_registry_owner]], [[rbrr_registry_name]], [[rbrr_ignite_machine_name]], [[rbrr_deploy_machine_name]], [[rbrr_crane_tar_gz]], [[rbrr_manifest_platforms]], [[rbrr_chosen_podman_version]], [[rbrr_chosen_vmimage_origin]], [[rbrr_chosen_identity]] anchors and their definitions wherever they appear in the file.

**IMPORTANT**: Before removing each attribute reference, search the file itself for uses of that attribute (e.g., `{at_stash_machine}`, `{ops_rbv_check}` etc.). Only remove if the attribute is not used elsewhere in the document outside its own definition. If it IS used elsewhere, leave a comment noting the dangling reference rather than silently removing.

## 2. RBRR-RegimeRepo.adoc (`/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/lenses/RBRR-RegimeRepo.adoc`)

Remove these three variable documentation sections (each starts with `.RBRR_VARNAME` and ends with `|===`):
- RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER section (around lines 74-84)
- RBRR_REGISTRY_NAME section (around lines 86-96)  
- RBRR_GITHUB_PAT_ENV section (around lines 98-108)

Also remove the `=== Registry Configuration` heading (around line 72) since it will have no content after these removals.

Read each file before editing. Don't change anything not specified above.

content.content:
1→= Base Configuration Regime
     2→:doctype: book
     3→
     4→// tag::mapping-section[]
     5→//
     6→// Category declarations:
     7→// at_: Architectural terms
     8→// st_: Support/infrastructure terms
     9→//
    10→// Domain declaration: conf_<canonical_term>
    11→
    12→:at_bottle_container:        <<term_bottle_container,Bottle Container>>
    13→:at_sentry_container:        <<term_sentry_container,Sentry Container>>
    14→
    15→:at_bottle_service:          <<term_bottle_service,Bottle Service>>
    16→:at_bottle_service_s:        <<term_bottle_service,Bottle Services>>
    17→:at_enclave_network:         <<term_enclave_network,Enclave Network>>
    18→:at_enclave_network_s:       <<term_enclave_network,Enclave Networks>>
    19→:at_nameplate:               <<term_nameplate,Nameplate>>
    20→:at_nameplate_s:             <<term_nameplate,Nameplates>>
    21→:at_rbm_system:              <<term_rbm_system,RBM System>>
    22→:at_container_registry:      <<term_container_registry,Remote Container Registry>>
    23→:st_container:               <<term_container,Container>>
    24→:st_container_s:             <<term_container,Containers>>
    25→:st_subnet:                  <<term_subnet,Subnet>>
    26→:st_gateway:                 <<term_gateway,Gateway>>
    27→:st_published_image_s:       <<term_published_image,Published Images>>
    28→
    29→// end::mapping-section[]
    30→
    31→== Base Configuration
    32→
    33→=== Network Configuration
    34→
    35→.RBRR_DNS_SERVER
    36→[cols="1,4"]
    37→|===
    38→|Purpose |Specifies the upstream DNS resolver for all
    39→{at_bottle_service_s}.
    40→Each
    41→{at_sentry_container}
    42→uses this DNS server to resolve domain names.
    43→The
    44→{at_bottle_container}
    45→never directly accesses this: the
    46→{at_sentry_container}
    47→acts on its behalf when permitted.
    48→
    49→|Required |No
    50→
    51→|Format |Valid IPv4 address
    52→
    53→|Example |export RBRR_DNS_SERVER := 8.8.8.8
    54→|===
    55→
    56→=== System Configuration
    57→
    58→.RBRR_NAMEPLATE_PATH
    59→[cols="1,4"]
    60→|===
    61→|Purpose |Defines the directory containing all {at_nameplate} definitions
    62→
    63→|Required |No
    64→
    65→|Format |Relative path from {at_rbm_system} root
    66→
    67→|Default |"nameplates"
    68→
    69→|Example |export RBRR_NAMEPLATE_PATH := recipes/nameplates
    70→|===
    71→
    72→=== Registry Configuration
    73→
    74→.RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER
    75→[cols="1,4"]
    76→|===
    77→|Purpose |Specifies the GitHub username or organization that owns the container registry namespace. This determines the registry path where {st_published_image_s} are stored.
    78→
    79→|Required |Yes
    80→
    81→|Format |Valid GitHub username or organization name
    82→
    83→|Example |export RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER := bhyslop
    84→|===
    85→
    86→.RBRR_REGISTRY_NAME
    87→[cols="1,4"]
    88→|===
    89→|Purpose |Defines the repository name within the GitHub container registry. Combined with RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER, this forms the complete registry path for storing {st_container} images.
    90→
    91→|Required |Yes
    92→
    93→|Format |Valid GitHub repository name
    94→
    95→|Example |export RBRR_REGISTRY_NAME := recipemuster
    96→|===
    97→
    98→.RBRR_GITHUB_PAT_ENV
    99→[cols="1,4"]
   100→|===
   101→|Purpose |Path to environment file containing GitHub credentials for API and {at_container_registry} authentication. Must contain RBV_USERNAME and RBV_PAT variables.
   102→
   103→|Required |Yes
   104→
   105→|Format |Relative or absolute file path
   106→
   107→|Example |export RBRR_GITHUB_PAT_ENV := ../station-files/secrets/github-ghcr-play.env
   108→|===
   109→
   110→== Term Definitions
   111→
   112→[[term_bottle_service]]
   113→{at_bottle_service}::
   114→A complete service instance consisting of one bottle container and one sentry container, working together to provide secure functionality. Each service maintains strict file system and network boundaries while enabling controlled access to resources.
   115→
   116→[[term_enclave_network]]
   117→{at_enclave_network}::
   118→An isolated network connecting a bottle container to its sentry container.
   119→This network enforces strict security policies and provides the only communication path for the bottle container.
   120→
   121→[[term_nameplate]]
   122→{at_nameplate}::
   123→A configuration file stored in
   124→RBRR_NAMEPLATE_PATH
   125→that defines all aspects of a bottle service, including its images, network settings, and security policies.
   126→Each nameplate provides a complete service specification that the RBM System can instantiate.
   127→
   128→[[term_rbm_system]]
   129→{at_rbm_system}::
   130→The complete Recipe Bottle Makefile system, consisting of the
   131→RBM Console,
   132→RBM Config Makefile,
   133→nameplates,
   134→and
   135→associated tools.
   136→This system enables secure deployment and management of containerized services.
   137→
   138→[[term_container_registry]]
   139→{at_container_registry}:: 
   140→A service that stores and provides access to published images.
   141→
   142→[[term_container]]
   143→{st_container}:: 
   144→A lightweight, standalone executable package containing all necessary components to run a service.
   145→
   146→[[term_subnet]]
   147→{st_subnet}:: 
   148→A logically visible subdivision of IP network address space.
   149→
   150→[[term_gateway]]
   151→{st_gateway}:: 
   152→A network node that serves as the entry point between two networks.
   153→
   154→[[term_published_image]]
   155→{st_published_image}:: 
   156→An image that has been uploaded to a container registry.
   157→
   158→

1→= Recipe Bottle Specification

870→
   871→[[mkr_command_exec]]
   872→// ⟦axl_voices axo_sequence axe_bash_interactive⟧
   873→=== {mkr_command_exec}
   874→
   875→include::RBSCE-command_exec.adoc[]
   876→
   877→=== VM Management Operations
   878→
   879→[[ops_rbv_check]]
   880→// ⟦axl_voices axo_command axe_bash_interactive⟧
   881→==== {ops_rbv_check}
   882→
   883→Verifies whether newer VM images are available upstream by comparing SHA digests.
   884→
   885→include::RBSVC-rbv_check.adoc[]
   886→
   887→[[ops_rbv_mirror]]
   888→// ⟦axl_voices axo_command axe_bash_interactive⟧
   889→==== {ops_rbv_mirror}
   890→
   891→Copies the chosen VM image from upstream to the user's GHCR, ensuring consistent deployment across environments.
   892→
   893→include::RBSVM-rbv_mirror.adoc[]
   894→
   895→[[mkc_interface_check]]
   896→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_scripted⟧
   897→==== {mkc_interface_check}
   898→
   899→A utility that verifies network interface presence and configuration within a container context:
   900→
   901→[source,makefile]
   902→----
   903→# Waits until interface has an IPv4 address assigned, fails after timeout
   904→# @param 1 interface   Network interface name to check
   905→# @param 2 timeout_s   Number of seconds to wait before failing
   906→define check_interface
   907→timeout $(2)s sh -c "while ! ip addr show $(1) | grep -q \"inet \"; do sleep 0.2; done"
   908→endef
   909→----
   910→
   911→This utility generates precise validation commands for network interface readiness probes, accepting an interface name and timeout duration as parameters.
   912→
   913→// ============================================================================
   914→// TIER 3: REFERENCE
   915→// ============================================================================
   916→
   917→== Term Definitions
   918→
   919→=== Core {gcp_project_id} Terms
   920→
   921→[[at_recipe_bottle]]
   922→{at_recipe_bottle}::
   923→The comprehensive container management platform that enables secure, role-based building, deployment, and retrieval of containerized applications through
   924→{gcp_service}
   925→infrastructure.
   926→
   927→=== RB Role Infrastructure
   928→
   929→[[rbtr_role]]
   930→// ⟦axl_voices axo_identity⟧
   931→{rbtr_role}::
   932→An operational identity within the
   933→{at_recipe_bottle}
   934→system
   935→that an
   936→{at_operator}
   937→can assume to perform specific cloud operations.
   938→Each
   939→{rbtr_role}
   940→is implemented through a dedicated
   941→{giam_service_account}
   942→and corresponding
   943→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
   944→containing the necessary credentials, with the exception of the
   945→{rbtr_mason}
   946→which operates exclusively within cloud infrastructure.
   947→
   948→=== {giam_service_account} Definitions
   949→
   950→[[rbtr_payor]]
   951→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   952→{rbtr_payor}::
   953→An
   954→{rbtr_role}
   955→that enables an
   956→{at_operator}
   957→to manage billing accounts and create depot projects using OAuth user authentication rather than service accounts.
   958→The
   959→{rbtr_payor}
   960→operates through OAuth 2.0 desktop application flow, enabling individual developers to manage Recipe Bottle infrastructure without requiring organization-level permissions.
   961→
   962→[[rbtr_governor]]
   963→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   964→{rbtr_governor}::
   965→An
   966→{rbtr_role}
   967→that grants an
   968→{at_operator}
   969→{gcp_project_id}

1560→{at_startup_script}::
  1561→A shell script that performs initialization and configuration tasks when starting a
  1562→{at_bottle_service}.
  1563→
  1564→[[at_stash_machine]]
  1565→{at_stash_machine}::
  1566→A temporary Podman virtual machine created with crane installed for performing registry queries and image operations.
  1567→Removed after completing its specific tasks.
  1568→
  1569→[[at_operational_machine]]
  1570→{at_operational_machine}::
  1571→The persistent Podman virtual machine that hosts all
  1572→{at_bottle_service_s}
  1573→and their associated containers.
  1574→
  1575→[[at_transit_network_name]]
  1576→{at_transit_network_name}::
  1577→The unique network name formed by appending `-uplink` to the service's
  1578→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1579→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-uplink`.
  1580→
  1581→[[at_enclave_network_name]]
  1582→{at_enclave_network_name}::
  1583→The unique network name formed by appending `-enclave` to the service's
  1584→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1585→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-enclave`.
  1586→
  1587→[[at_enclave_namespace_name]]
  1588→{at_enclave_namespace_name}::
  1589→The unique namespace name formed by appending `-namespace` to the service's
  1590→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1591→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-namespace`.
  1592→
  1593→[[at_bottle_container_name]]
  1594→{at_bottle_container_name}::
  1595→The unique container name formed by appending `-bottle` to the service's
  1596→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1597→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-bottle`.
  1598→
  1599→[[at_sentry_container_name]]
  1600→{at_sentry_container_name}::
  1601→The unique container name formed by appending `-sentry` to the service's
  1602→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1603→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-sentry`.
  1604→
  1605→[[st_dockerfile]]
  1606→{st_dockerfile}::
  1607→A specification file that defines how to build a
  1608→{st_image}.
  1609→

70→|===
    71→
    72→=== Registry Configuration
    73→
    74→.RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER
    75→[cols="1,4"]
    76→|===
    77→|Purpose |Specifies the GitHub username or organization that owns the container registry namespace. This determines the registry path where {st_published_image_s} are stored.
    78→
    79→|Required |Yes
    80→
    81→|Format |Valid GitHub username or organization name
    82→
    83→|Example |export RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER := bhyslop
    84→|===
    85→
    86→.RBRR_REGISTRY_NAME
    87→[cols="1,4"]
    88→|===
    89→|Purpose |Defines the repository name within the GitHub container registry. Combined with RBRR_REGISTRY_OWNER, this forms the complete registry path for storing {st_container} images.
    90→
    91→|Required |Yes
    92→
    93→|Format |Valid GitHub repository name
    94→
    95→|Example |export RBRR_REGISTRY_NAME := recipemuster
    96→|===
    97→
    98→.RBRR_GITHUB_PAT_ENV
    99→[cols="1,4"]
   100→|===
   101→|Purpose |Path to environment file containing GitHub credentials for API and {at_container_registry} authentication. Must contain RBV_USERNAME and RBV_PAT variables.
   102→
   103→|Required |Yes
   104→
   105→|Format |Relative or absolute file path
   106→
   107→|Example |export RBRR_GITHUB_PAT_ENV := ../station-files/secrets/github-ghcr-play.env
   108→|===
   109→
   110→== Term Definitions
   111→
   112→[[term_bottle_service]]
   113→{at_bottle_service}::
   114→A complete service instance consisting of one bottle container and one sentry container, working together to provide secure functionality. Each service maintains strict file system and network boundaries while enabling controlled access to resources.
   115→
   116→[[term_enclave_network]]
   117→{at_enclave_network}::
   118→An isolated network connecting a bottle container to its sentry container.
   119→This network enforces strict security policies and provides the only communication path for the bottle container.

|Required |Yes

|Format |Valid GitHub repository name

|Example |export RBRR_REGISTRY_NAME := recipemuster

.RBRR_GITHUB_PAT_ENV
[cols="1,4"]
|===

|Required |Yes

|Format |Relative or absolute file path

|Example |export RBRR_GITHUB_PAT_ENV := ../station-files/secrets/github-ghcr-play.env
|===

870→==== {mkc_interface_check}
   871→
   872→A utility that verifies network interface presence and configuration within a container context:
   873→
   874→[source,makefile]
   875→----
   876→# Waits until interface has an IPv4 address assigned, fails after timeout
   877→# @param 1 interface   Network interface name to check
   878→# @param 2 timeout_s   Number of seconds to wait before failing
   879→define check_interface
   880→timeout $(2)s sh -c "while ! ip addr show $(1) | grep -q \"inet \"; do sleep 0.2; done"
   881→endef
   882→----
   883→
   884→This utility generates precise validation commands for network interface readiness probes, accepting an interface name and timeout duration as parameters.
   885→
   886→// ============================================================================
   887→// TIER 3: REFERENCE
   888→// ============================================================================
   889→
   890→== Term Definitions
   891→
   892→=== Core {gcp_project_id} Terms
   893→
   894→[[at_recipe_bottle]]
   895→{at_recipe_bottle}::
   896→The comprehensive container management platform that enables secure, role-based building, deployment, and retrieval of containerized applications through
   897→{gcp_service}
   898→infrastructure.
   899→

1556→{at_bottle_container_name}::
  1557→The unique container name formed by appending `-bottle` to the service's
  1558→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1559→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-bottle`.
  1560→
  1561→[[at_sentry_container_name]]
  1562→{at_sentry_container_name}::
  1563→The unique container name formed by appending `-sentry` to the service's
  1564→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1565→Pattern: `${RBRN_MONIKER}-sentry`.
  1566→
  1567→[[st_dockerfile]]
  1568→{st_dockerfile}::
  1569→A specification file that defines how to build a
  1570→{st_image}.
  1571→
  1572→[[st_published_image]]
  1573→{st_published_image}::
  1574→A
  1575→{st_image}
  1576→that has been uploaded to a
  1577→{at_container_registry}.
  1578→
  1579→[[st_image]]
  1580→{st_image}::
  1581→A template used to create
  1582→{st_container}
  1583→instances, containing the service's code, runtime, and dependencies.
  1584→
  1585→[[st_image_store]]

68→
    69→|Example |export RBRR_NAMEPLATE_PATH := recipes/nameplates
    70→|===
    71→
    72→== Term Definitions
    73→
    74→[[term_bottle_service]]
    75→{at_bottle_service}::
    76→A complete service instance consisting of one bottle container and one sentry container, working together to provide secure functionality. Each service maintains strict file system and network boundaries while enabling controlled access to resources.
    77→
    78→[[term_enclave_network]]
    79→{at_enclave_network}::
    80→An isolated network connecting a bottle container to its sentry container.
    81→This network enforces strict security policies and provides the only communication path for the bottle container.
    82→
    83→[[term_nameplate]]
    84→{at_nameplate}::
    85→A configuration file stored in
    86→RBRR_NAMEPLATE_PATH
    87→that defines all aspects of a bottle service, including its images, network settings, and security policies.
    88→Each nameplate provides a complete service specification that the RBM System can instantiate.
    89→
    90→[[term_rbm_system]]
    91→{at_rbm_system}::
    92→The complete Recipe Bottle Makefile system, consisting of the
    93→RBM Console,
    94→RBM Config Makefile,
    95→nameplates,
    96→and
    97→associated tools.
    98→This system enables secure deployment and management of containerized services.
    99→
   100→[[term_container_registry]]
   101→{at_container_registry}:: 
   102→A service that stores and provides access to published images.
   103→
   104→[[term_container]]
   105→{st_container}:: 
   106→A lightweight, standalone executable package containing all necessary components to run a service.
   107→
   108→[[term_subnet]]
   109→{st_subnet}:: 
   110→A logically visible subdivision of IP network address space.
   111→
   112→[[term_gateway]]
   113→{st_gateway}:: 
   114→A network node that serves as the entry point between two networks.
   115→
   116→[[term_published_image]]
   117→{st_published_image}::
